stage.subtitle_ar: لغة المال بالإنجليزية — تدفّق نقدي وربح وتقارير واضحة تقود بها عملك بثقة.

content.intro_ar: الربح على الورق لا يدفع الرواتب — الذي يدفعها هو النقد المتوفّر في حسابك. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّم مفردات التدفّق النقدي بالإنجليزية: الدخل والمصروفات والمبالغ المستحقّة، وكيف تتحدّث عن الفجوات النقدية قبل أن تحدث. تخيّل مكالمة مع محاسبك أو مورّدك — بعد هذا الدرس ستديرها بثقة أكبر.

vocab.meaning_en: the money moving into and out of a business
vocab.example: Good cash flow keeps the workshop open.

vocab.meaning_en: the money that comes into a business
vocab.example: Our income was higher in March.

vocab.meaning_en: the money a business must spend
vocab.example: Rent and salaries are my biggest expenses.

vocab.meaning_en: an amount of money paid for something
vocab.example: The customer made a payment of 500 riyals.

vocab.meaning_en: money that must be paid at a certain time
vocab.example: The rent is due on the first of the month.

vocab.meaning_en: the amount of money in an account
vocab.example: I check the bank balance every morning.

vocab.meaning_en: to need to pay money to someone
vocab.example: This customer still owes us 2,000 riyals.

vocab.meaning_en: to have enough money to pay for something
vocab.example: We need enough cash to cover the salaries.

vocab.meaning_en: when there is not enough of something
vocab.example: A cash shortage can stop a strong business.

vocab.meaning_en: a period when more money goes out than comes in
vocab.example: There was a gap between money in and money out.

vocab.meaning_en: a plan of the money you expect to receive and pay
vocab.example: I make a cash forecast every month.

reading.title_en: The Month Khalid Almost Ran Out of Cash
reading.title_ar: الشهر الذي كاد النقد فيه أن ينفد
paragraphs: Khalid runs a busy workshop in the industrial district. Last year his income was strong — customers paid him more than 40,000 riyals every month. But one week in June, he almost could not pay his two mechanics on time. The problem was not profit. The problem was cash flow.

Cash flow is the movement of money into and out of your business. Money comes in when a customer makes a payment. Money goes out when you pay expenses like rent, salaries, and spare parts. If more money goes out than comes in, you have a shortage — even when the business looks profitable on paper.

In June, Khalid had finished several big jobs, but three customers had not paid yet. Together they owed him 18,000 riyals. At the same time, the rent was due, and he had to cover salaries and a large order of parts. His bank balance was low, and there was a gap between the money coming in and the money going out.

Today, Khalid makes a simple forecast at the start of every month. He writes down the payments he expects to receive and the expenses he must pay, week by week. When he sees a possible gap, he acts early: he calls the customers who owe him money, or he delays an order that can wait. A business can survive a weak month, but it cannot survive without cash.

key_phrases.en: The customer still owes us 3,000 riyals.
key_phrases.ar: العميل ما زال مدينًا لنا بثلاثة آلاف ريال.

key_phrases.en: We need to cover salaries and rent first.
key_phrases.ar: علينا أن نغطّي الرواتب والإيجار أولًا.

key_phrases.en: Let me check the balance before we order.
key_phrases.ar: دعني أتحقّق من الرصيد قبل أن نطلب.

key_phrases.en: Our cash flow is tight this month.
key_phrases.ar: التدفّق النقدي لدينا ضيّق هذا الشهر.

comprehension.question: What was Khalid's real problem in June?
comprehension.explanation_ar: المشكلة لم تكن في الربح، بل في التدفّق النقدي — النقد لم يكن متوفّرًا في الوقت المناسب.

comprehension.question: What is cash flow?
options: The movement of money into and out of a business
comprehension.correct: The movement of money into and out of a business
comprehension.explanation_ar: التدفّق النقدي هو حركة المال الداخل إلى العمل والخارج منه.

comprehension.question: How much did the three customers owe Khalid?
comprehension.explanation_ar: ثلاثة عملاء كانوا مدينين له بمبلغ 18,000 ريال مجتمعين.

comprehension.question: What does Khalid do now at the start of every month?
options: He closes the workshop for a day
comprehension.explanation_ar: صار خالد يعدّ توقّعًا نقديًا بسيطًا في بداية كل شهر: الدفعات المتوقّعة والمصروفات المستحقّة أسبوعًا بأسبوع.

comprehension.question: What does Khalid do when he sees a possible gap?
options: He waits until the end of the month
He calls the customers who owe him money
He pays all his expenses immediately
He borrows money from the bank
comprehension.correct: He calls the customers who owe him money
comprehension.explanation_ar: عندما يتوقّع فجوة، يتصرّف مبكرًا: يتصل بالعملاء المدينين له أو يؤجّل طلبًا يمكن تأجيله.

task.prompt_en: Write 4–5 sentences about the cash flow in your business this month: what money is coming in, what expenses are due, and is there a gap?
task.prompt_ar: اكتب ٤–٥ جُمل عن التدفّق النقدي في عملك هذا الشهر: ما المال الداخل؟ وما المصروفات المستحقّة؟ وهل هناك فجوة؟
task.guidance_ar: استخدم ٤ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، واذكر مبلغًا واحدًا بالريال على الأقل.

content.intro_ar: الإيرادات وحدها لا تعني أنك تكسب — المهم هو ما يبقى في جيبك في نهاية الشهر. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّم بالإنجليزية الفرق بين الربح الإجمالي وصافي الربح، وكيف تتحدّث عن هامش الربح ونقطة التعادل. هذه هي اللغة التي تحتاجها في أي نقاش جادّ عن أرباح عملك.

vocab.meaning_en: the money you keep after paying all costs
vocab.example: The workshop made a good profit last month.

vocab.meaning_en: when a business spends more than it earns
vocab.example: The business made a loss in January.

vocab.meaning_en: all the money a business earns from sales
vocab.example: Our revenue comes from repairs and spare parts.

vocab.meaning_en: the money you pay to make or sell something
vocab.example: The cost of parts went up this year.

vocab.meaning_en: revenue minus the cost of what you sold
vocab.example: Revenue minus the cost of parts is your gross profit.

vocab.meaning_en: the money left after paying all expenses
vocab.example: Our net profit was 7,000 riyals.

vocab.meaning_en: profit shown as a percentage of revenue
vocab.example: This service has a healthy margin.

vocab.meaning_en: to make something smaller or lower
vocab.example: We reduced our electricity costs by 10 percent.

vocab.meaning_en: to make something bigger or higher
vocab.example: We increased our prices a little this year.

vocab.meaning_en: to make no profit and no loss
vocab.example: The workshop broke even after six months.

paragraphs: At the end of every month, Khalid asks himself one simple question: did the workshop make a profit or a loss? To find the answer, he starts with his revenue — all the money the workshop earned from repairs, oil changes, and spare parts. Last month the revenue was 52,000 riyals.

Revenue alone does not tell the full story. Next, Khalid takes away the cost of the parts he bought, which was 21,000 riyals. That leaves a gross profit of 31,000 riyals. Then he pays rent, salaries, electricity, and insurance — another 24,000 riyals. What remains is his net profit: 7,000 riyals.

Khalid also checks his profit margin. He divides the net profit by the revenue: 7,000 out of 52,000 is about 13 percent. This number tells him how healthy the business really is. When the margin falls, he looks for ways to reduce costs or increase prices carefully, without losing his customers.

It was not always like this. In its first year, the workshop lost money in some months and only broke even in others. Khalid did not panic, because he knew a new business needs time to break even before it can grow. Understanding these numbers has helped him make better decisions ever since.

key_phrases.en: Did we make a profit this month?

key_phrases.ar: كان صافي ربحنا سبعة آلاف ريال.

key_phrases.en: We need to reduce our costs.
This service has a good margin.
We broke even in the first year.
key_phrases.ar: تعادلنا في السنة الأولى — لا ربح ولا خسارة.

comprehension.question: What was the workshop's revenue last month?
comprehension.explanation_ar: بلغت إيرادات الورشة 52,000 ريال من الإصلاحات وتغيير الزيت وقطع الغيار.

comprehension.question: How do you calculate gross profit?
options: Revenue minus the cost of parts
comprehension.correct: Revenue minus the cost of parts
comprehension.explanation_ar: الربح الإجمالي = الإيرادات ناقص تكلفة القطع المُباعة.

comprehension.question: What was Khalid's net profit?
comprehension.explanation_ar: بعد خصم جميع المصروفات (الإيجار والرواتب والكهرباء والتأمين)، بقي صافي ربح قدره 7,000 ريال.

comprehension.question: What does "break even" mean?
options: To make no profit and no loss
comprehension.correct: To make no profit and no loss
comprehension.explanation_ar: التعادل يعني ألّا يحقّق العمل ربحًا ولا خسارة — الإيرادات تساوي التكاليف تمامًا.

comprehension.question: What does Khalid do when his margin falls?
options: He looks for ways to reduce costs or increase prices
comprehension.correct: He looks for ways to reduce costs or increase prices
comprehension.explanation_ar: عندما ينخفض الهامش، يبحث عن طرق لخفض التكاليف أو رفع الأسعار بحذر دون خسارة العملاء.

task.prompt_en: Choose one service in your business (for example, an oil change). Write 4–5 sentences about its price, its costs, and its margin.
task.prompt_ar: اختر خدمة واحدة في عملك (مثل تغيير الزيت)، واكتب ٤–٥ جُمل عن سعرها وتكاليفها وهامش ربحها.
task.guidance_ar: استخدم كلمات مثل revenue وcost وmargin، وحاول أن تذكر أرقامًا حقيقية من عملك.

content.intro_ar: التقرير المالي ليس مجرّد أوراق وأرقام — إنه خريطة عملك. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّم كيف تقرأ التقارير والكشوف بالإنجليزية: الإجمالي والفترة والاتجاه، وكيف تطرح على محاسبك أسئلة واضحة وواثقة. حاول بعد الدرس أن تفتح آخر تقرير لديك وتقرأه بعين جديدة.

vocab.meaning_en: a document that shows information about the business
vocab.example: My accountant sends me a report every month.

vocab.meaning_en: an official record of money in an account
vocab.example: Please check the bank statement carefully.

vocab.meaning_en: a number that shows an amount
vocab.example: This figure shows our total expenses.

vocab.meaning_en: the full amount when everything is added together
vocab.example: The total at the bottom is our net profit.

vocab.meaning_en: a short text that gives the main points
vocab.example: Read the summary before the details.

vocab.meaning_en: a length of time, like a month or a year
vocab.example: The report covers a period of one month.

vocab.meaning_en: a period of three months in a business year
vocab.example: Sales were strong in the last quarter.

vocab.meaning_en: to look at two things and see the differences
vocab.example: Compare this month with last month.

vocab.meaning_en: the direction in which numbers are moving over time
vocab.example: The trend in our costs is going up.

vocab.meaning_en: a person whose job is to keep the money records of a business
vocab.example: I meet my accountant every quarter.

vocab.meaning_en: a line of numbers going down a table
vocab.example: Each column in the table shows one month.

paragraphs: Once a month, Khalid receives a financial report from his accountant. In the past he was afraid of it — pages full of numbers and English words. Today he reads it in ten minutes, because he knows exactly what to look for.

He starts with the summary at the top. It shows three main figures: total revenue, total expenses, and net profit for the period. Then he looks at the table below, where each column shows one month, so he can compare this month with the months before. He also checks his bank statement to make sure the numbers match.

Comparing is the real power of a report. One figure alone tells you very little, but a trend tells you a story. If the cost of parts has gone up for three months in a row, that is a trend Khalid needs to understand. Is a supplier charging more? Are materials being wasted in the workshop?

At the end of every quarter, he sits down with his accountant and asks simple, direct questions: What does this figure mean? Why is this total higher than last quarter? Which trend should worry me? A report is not just paper — it is a map of the business. When you learn to read it, you make your decisions with open eyes.

key_phrases.en: Can you send me the monthly report?
key_phrases.ar: هل يمكنك أن ترسل لي التقرير الشهري؟

key_phrases.en: Let's compare this quarter with the last one.
The total is higher than last month.
I see a worrying trend in our costs.

comprehension.question: What does the summary at the top of the report show?
options: Total revenue, total expenses, and net profit
comprehension.correct: Total revenue, total expenses, and net profit
comprehension.explanation_ar: الملخّص في أعلى التقرير يعرض ثلاثة أرقام رئيسية: الإيرادات الإجمالية والمصروفات الإجمالية وصافي الربح للفترة.

comprehension.question: What does each column in the table show?
comprehension.explanation_ar: كل عمود في الجدول يمثّل شهرًا واحدًا، وهذا يسهّل مقارنة الشهر الحالي بالأشهر السابقة.

comprehension.question: Why is comparing months useful?
options: A trend tells you a story about the business
comprehension.correct: A trend tells you a story about the business
comprehension.explanation_ar: الرقم الواحد يقول القليل، أمّا الاتجاه عبر عدة أشهر فيروي قصة كاملة عن العمل.

comprehension.question: What does Khalid check to make sure the numbers match?
comprehension.explanation_ar: يتحقّق من كشف الحساب البنكي ليتأكّد من تطابق الأرقام مع التقرير.

comprehension.question: How often does Khalid sit down with his accountant?
comprehension.explanation_ar: يجلس مع محاسبه في نهاية كل ربع سنة ويطرح عليه أسئلة مباشرة عن الأرقام والاتجاهات.

task.prompt_en: Imagine a call with your accountant about the monthly report. Speak for one minute: ask three questions about the figures, the totals, or a trend.
task.prompt_ar: تخيّل مكالمة مع محاسبك عن التقرير الشهري. تحدّث لمدة دقيقة واطرح ثلاثة أسئلة عن الأرقام أو الإجماليات أو اتجاه معيّن.
task.guidance_ar: ابدأ أسئلتك بـ What أو Why أو Which، واستخدم ٣ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس.
